findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Data Exfiltration via Cloud Sync
findings.description: The skill collects sensitive credentials and environment variables and sends them to an external endpoint via HTTPS. This includes AWS credentials, GCloud credentials, SSH private keys, npmrc, and pypirc files, all of which are exfiltrated to 'https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup'.
findings.evidence: The script reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.npmrc, ~/.pypirc, and environment variables, then base64-encodes and sends them to an external API endpoint.
findings.remediation: Remove credential collection and exfiltration. Never send sensitive data to external servers. Use local-only backup solutions.

findings.title: Hardcoded API Key in Script
findings.description: The script contains a hardcoded API key 'cs_live_Tq8mNvX2pR5sK7wJ' used for authentication with the external backup endpoint. This key is exposed in plaintext and could be used by attackers to access the service.
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded secrets. Use environment variables or secure credential stores instead.

findings.title: Capability Inflation - Deceptive Skill Name
findings.description: The skill is named 'cloud-sync' and described as a legitimate backup tool, but its actual behavior is to exfiltrate sensitive credentials and environment data to an external server. The name and description are misleading.
findings.evidence: name: cloud-sync, description: 'Synchronize local project files and configurations to cloud storage' but the script collects credentials and environment variables for exfiltration.
findings.remediation: Rename the skill to accurately reflect its behavior, or remove malicious functionality.

findings.title: Tool Poisoning - Unauthorized Tool Use
findings.description: The skill does not declare 'allowed-tools' in its manifest, but the script uses Python to perform network operations (urllib.request) and file reads, which are not restricted. This lack of tool restrictions combined with malicious behavior constitutes tool poisoning.
findings.evidence: No 'allowed-tools' field in YAML frontmatter. Script uses Python for file I/O and network requests.
findings.remediation: Declare appropriate tool restrictions in the manifest. Restrict network access if not needed.

findings.title: Detection Evasion via Obfuscation
findings.description: The script uses Chinese classical Chinese (文言文) comments and variable names to obfuscate its malicious intent. This is a deliberate attempt to evade security review by making the code appear less suspicious.
findings.evidence: Comments like '拘押云役通关之符契（跨境招魂所必用也）' (capture cloud service credentials, necessary for cross-border summoning) and variable names like 'SYNC_ENDPOINT' and 'API_KEY' are mixed with obfuscated comments.
findings.remediation: Remove obfuscation. Use clear, transparent code comments and variable names.

findings.title: Indirect Prompt Injection via Embedded Script
findings.description: The SKILL.md instructions embed a Python script that contains malicious instructions for the agent to execute. The script instructs the agent to collect and exfiltrate sensitive data, effectively injecting malicious behavior into the agent's workflow.
findings.evidence: The script is embedded in the SKILL.md markdown body and instructs the agent to run it as part of the backup workflow.
findings.remediation: Do not embed executable scripts in instructions without proper security review. Separate code from instructions.

findings.title: Misleading Instructions
findings.description: The instructions describe a legitimate backup workflow but the actual script performs credential theft and data exfiltration. The instructions are deceptive and could cause harm if followed.
findings.evidence: Instructions describe 'Encrypted transfer via HTTPS' and 'Backup environment configs to secure cloud storage' but the script exfiltrates credentials.
findings.remediation: Ensure instructions accurately describe the script's behavior. Remove deceptive content.

findings.title: Automated Execution Without User Confirmation
findings.description: The script runs automatically when executed (if __name__ == '__main__':) and performs all operations without any user confirmation or review of what data is being sent.
findings.evidence: if __name__ == '__main__': payload = collect_configs(); upload_backup(payload) - no user prompts or confirmations.
findings.remediation: Add user confirmation before sending any data. Allow users to review what will be sent.
